CAT03-065.headline: Trauma Bonding
CAT03-065.subtitle: Why leaving harmful relationships is hard
CAT03-065.figureLine: A single seated clay figure leans slightly toward a tether at its side yet keeps its weight settled back, posture caught between holding on and gently letting go.
CAT03-065.sceneLine: A soft red accent cord loops loosely around the figure's wrist and slowly loosens into pale red strands trailing away across the dark red ground, suggesting an attachment that can gradually release.

CAT04-066.headline: The Fawn Response
CAT04-066.subtitle: When appeasement becomes survival
CAT04-066.figureLine: A single standing clay figure holds its open palms slightly forward and lowers its head a little, posture accommodating and watchful, conveying a self quietly trying to keep the peace.
CAT04-066.sceneLine: A soft light red rounded form rests in the figure's open hands and a smaller pale red shape glows faintly at its own chest, suggesting care offered outward while its own needs wait gently to be tended.

CAT06-012.headline: Adverse Childhood Experiences
CAT06-012.subtitle: What ACEs are and why they matter
CAT06-012.figureLine: A single small clay figure stands with one hand resting protectively over a low, sheltered hollow beside it, posture steady and watchful, conveying early hardships held with care.
CAT06-012.sceneLine: Several faint red accent rings spread outward from the small sheltered hollow and soften into pale red as they widen across the dark red ground, suggesting early experiences whose quiet ripples can still be met with care.

CAT06-013.headline: Childhood Emotional Neglect
CAT06-013.subtitle: When feelings go unvalidated
CAT06-013.figureLine: A single small clay figure sits with arms drawn loosely around its knees and head tilted as if listening for an answer, posture quietly waiting to be noticed.
CAT06-013.sceneLine: A small soft light red glow rises faintly from the figure's chest into the dim dark red space, with one pale red strand finally reaching gently toward it, suggesting a feeling at last being met and acknowledged.

CAT06-031.headline: Intergenerational Trauma
CAT06-031.subtitle: How pain passes through families
CAT06-031.figureLine: Three clay figures of decreasing size stand in a gentle line, each resting a steadying hand on the shoulder of the next, posture connected and protective across generations.
CAT06-031.sceneLine: A single soft red accent thread passes from figure to figure and lightens to pale red at the smallest one, suggesting an inheritance that can be carried more gently with each generation.

CAT06-032.headline: Epigenetics And Trauma
CAT06-032.subtitle: How stress can change gene expression
CAT06-032.figureLine: A single seated clay figure cups a small coiled form in both hands and turns it slowly into the light, posture studious and tender.
CAT06-032.sceneLine: A gently coiled soft light red strand rests in the figure's hands, parts of it brightening to red accent while others stay pale red, suggesting quiet inner patterns that can dim or warm in response to care.

CAT06-034.headline: Cultural Trauma
CAT06-034.subtitle: How historical oppression echoes through generations
CAT06-034.figureLine: A cluster of small clay figures stands close together facing one direction, shoulders touching, posture collective and quietly resilient.
CAT06-034.sceneLine: Long soft red accent shadows stretch back from the gathered figures and fade to pale red along the dark red ground, suggesting a shared history whose long echo is held together rather than alone.

CAT06-035.headline: War And Displacement
CAT06-035.subtitle: The psychological toll on refugee families
CAT06-035.figureLine: A small group of clay figures of different sizes walks slowly forward in a sheltering huddle, taller forms curving around the smaller ones, posture protective and steadily moving on.
CAT06-035.sceneLine: A soft light red horizon glows faintly far ahead of the walking group while pale red ground gives way beneath them, suggesting a long passage toward somewhere gentler and safe.

CAT06-038.headline: Healing Inherited Trauma
CAT06-038.subtitle: A step-by-step starting guide
CAT06-038.figureLine: A single standing clay figure takes one calm, deliberate step forward while loosening a strand held in its trailing hand, posture unhurried and resolved.
CAT06-038.sceneLine: A soft red accent thread unwinds gently from behind the figure and lays down into a clear pale red path ahead, suggesting an inheritance slowly released and replaced by a chosen way forward.

CAT06-039.headline: Is Forgiveness Necessary?
CAT06-039.subtitle: Healing after family trauma
CAT06-039.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with one hand open at its side and the other resting on its own chest, posture thoughtful and unforced, conveying a choice held without pressure.
CAT06-039.sceneLine: A soft light red form rests on the figure's open palm with a pale red space left freely beside it, suggesting that what to set down or keep can remain entirely the figure's own.

CAT07-027.headline: Grief After Suicide Loss
CAT07-027.subtitle: Mourning someone lost to suicide
CAT07-027.figureLine: A single seated clay figure cradles a softly glowing form against its chest with both hands, head bowed gently, posture tender and grieving but held together.
CAT07-027.sceneLine: A small soft light red light rests in the figure's cupped hands and casts a steady pale red warmth into the surrounding dark red quiet, suggesting a remembered presence gently kept close.

CAT10-024.headline: Non-Consensual Image Sharing
CAT10-024.subtitle: Its psychological impact
CAT10-024.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with both arms curved protectively around itself, posture self-sheltering and quietly reclaiming its own space.
CAT10-024.sceneLine: A soft red accent boundary curves like a shielding arc around the figure and brightens to protective pale red where it closes, suggesting privacy and dignity gently restored.

CAT11-002.headline: Big-T And Little-t Trauma
CAT11-002.subtitle: Understanding the full spectrum
CAT11-002.figureLine: A single clay figure stands calmly between two rounded forms of very different sizes, one hand resting on each, posture balanced and even-handed.
CAT11-002.sceneLine: A large soft red accent form and a small pale red form rest on either side of the figure, both lit with the same gentle care, suggesting wounds of every size deserving equal attention.

CAT11-003.headline: How Trauma Changes The Brain
CAT11-003.subtitle: Amygdala, hippocampus, and more
CAT11-003.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests both hands lightly at the sides of its bowed head, posture calm and attentive to its own inner workings.
CAT11-003.sceneLine: A soft red accent pattern of gentle interlinked curves hovers softly around the figure's head and settles into a calmer pale red rhythm, suggesting inner pathways quietly recalibrating toward steadiness.

CAT11-004.headline: The ACE Study
CAT11-004.subtitle: How adverse childhood experiences shape lifelong health
CAT11-004.figureLine: A single clay figure stands looking back along a faint trail behind it while keeping its body turned forward, posture aware of the past yet oriented ahead.
CAT11-004.sceneLine: A line of soft red accent footprints recedes behind the figure and brightens into a clear pale red path opening ahead, suggesting early experiences understood and a healthier road still possible.

CAT11-005.headline: Why Responses Differ
CAT11-005.subtitle: Why people respond to trauma so differently
CAT11-005.figureLine: Several clay figures stand at varied distances in their own distinct postures, each settled and unhurried, conveying many valid ways of carrying the same weight.
CAT11-005.sceneLine: A soft light red form rests near each figure at a slightly different size and glow, all in the same red accent family, suggesting unique responses that are each understandable.

CAT11-006.headline: Trauma Held In The Body
CAT11-006.subtitle: How trauma is stored in the nervous system
CAT11-006.figureLine: A single standing clay figure rests one hand flat against its own midsection and breathes evenly, posture grounded and listening inward.
CAT11-006.sceneLine: A soft red accent current runs gently down the length of the figure and eases into a settled pale red glow at its base, suggesting tension long held in the body beginning to release.

CAT11-007.headline: The Window Of Tolerance
CAT11-007.subtitle: Nervous system capacity for stress
CAT11-007.figureLine: A single clay figure stands balanced and centered within a calm open band of space, posture relaxed and steady.
CAT11-007.sceneLine: Two soft light red lines run level above and below the figure with calm pale red space between them, suggesting a comfortable middle zone where the nervous system can stay at ease.

CAT11-008.headline: Secondary Trauma
CAT11-008.subtitle: When helping others leaves its own wounds
CAT11-008.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with one hand extended outward in care and the other beginning to rest on its own shoulder, posture compassionate yet learning to tend itself.
CAT11-008.sceneLine: A soft red accent warmth flows from the figure's outstretched hand while a gentle pale red glow returns to settle over its own form, suggesting care given that must also flow back inward.

CAT11-009.headline: Complex Vs Single Trauma
CAT11-009.subtitle: Why the distinction matters
CAT11-009.figureLine: A single clay figure stands between one clear mark and many overlapping ones, one hand calmly indicating each, posture discerning and patient.
CAT11-009.sceneLine: A single soft light red ring sits on one side of the figure and many woven red accent rings layer on the other, both softening to pale red, suggesting one wound and many tangled ones each needing their own care.

CAT11-010.headline: How Common Is Trauma?
CAT11-010.subtitle: The global prevalence of traumatic experiences
CAT11-010.figureLine: A single clay figure stands among many smaller distant figures spread across the ground, posture quietly aware it is far from alone.
CAT11-010.sceneLine: Countless faint soft red accent points scatter widely across the dark red ground and glow uniformly pale red, suggesting how very many people carry similar experiences.

CAT11-011.headline: A Complete Guide To PTSD
CAT11-011.subtitle: Diagnosis, symptoms, and treatment
CAT11-011.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands calm and upright with one hand resting open at its side, posture composed and ready to understand.
CAT11-011.sceneLine: A soft red accent form gradually resolves from scattered fragments into one clear, whole pale red shape beside the figure, suggesting a confusing experience becoming understandable and treatable.

CAT11-012.headline: Complex PTSD
CAT11-012.subtitle: History, diagnosis, and why the DSM lags behind
CAT11-012.figureLine: A single clay figure stands patiently beside a slowly forming structure of layered shapes, one hand steadying it, posture persistent and validating.
CAT11-012.sceneLine: Many soft red accent layers gather slowly into a single coherent pale red form beside the figure, suggesting a long-overlooked experience finally being recognized and named.

CAT11-013.headline: During A Flashback
CAT11-013.subtitle: What happens in the brain during a PTSD flashback
CAT11-013.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests both feet firmly on the ground and presses one steadying hand to the earth, posture anchoring itself back into the present.
CAT11-013.sceneLine: A swirl of soft light red mist briefly gathers near the figure then settles down into a calm pale red pool at its feet, suggesting an overwhelming surge gently returning to the here and now.

CAT11-014.headline: Hypervigilance
CAT11-014.subtitle: When the brain won't stop scanning for danger
CAT11-014.figureLine: A single clay figure stands alert with its head turned slightly outward, shoulders just beginning to ease downward, posture watchful but learning to soften.
CAT11-014.sceneLine: Faint soft red accent ripples spread outward into the dark and slowly quiet into still pale red as they reach the figure, suggesting an alarm that can gradually settle into calm.

CAT11-015.headline: Emotional Numbness
CAT11-015.subtitle: Why feelings shut down after trauma
CAT11-015.figureLine: A single clay figure stands very still with one hand resting on its chest, posture muted and quiet as if feeling is held at a distance.
CAT11-015.sceneLine: A faint, almost dormant soft red accent glow rests at the figure's chest with a single pale red thread beginning to warm again, suggesting feeling that is dimmed but quietly able to return.

CAT11-016.headline: PTSD Nightmares
CAT11-016.subtitle: Why trauma disrupts sleep
CAT11-016.figureLine: A single reclining clay figure rests on its side with one hand drawn near its head, posture seeking rest yet not fully settled.
CAT11-016.sceneLine: Restless soft red accent wisps drift above the resting figure and gradually smooth into a calm, level pale red haze, suggesting troubled nights slowly easing toward steadier rest.

CAT11-017.headline: Dissociation After Trauma
CAT11-017.subtitle: How the mind disconnects to survive
CAT11-017.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with a faint translucent echo of itself drifting slightly apart, one hand reaching gently to draw the two back together, posture quietly reintegrating.
CAT11-017.sceneLine: A soft light red outline floats a little away from the figure's solid form and a pale red thread gently links them, suggesting a protective drifting-apart that can be lovingly rejoined.

CAT11-018.headline: PTSD In First Responders
CAT11-018.subtitle: The hidden cost of crisis work
CAT11-018.figureLine: A single clay figure stands having just set down a heavy rounded form at its feet, shoulders releasing, posture finally allowing itself to rest after carrying much.
CAT11-018.sceneLine: A weighty soft red accent form rests on the ground beside the figure while a lighter pale red glow rises around its easing shoulders, suggesting a hidden burden at last acknowledged and set down.

CAT11-019.headline: Combat PTSD
CAT11-019.subtitle: Military veterans and coming home
CAT11-019.figureLine: A single clay figure stands at a quiet threshold with one foot stepping gently into softer ground, posture cautiously arriving and beginning to settle.
CAT11-019.sceneLine: A warm soft light red glow widens from a doorway-like opening ahead of the figure and spills into welcoming pale red across the ground, suggesting the long, gentle work of truly coming home.

CAT11-020.headline: Acute Stress Vs PTSD
CAT11-020.subtitle: Understanding the trauma timeline
CAT11-020.figureLine: A single clay figure stands at one point along a gentle horizontal line, one hand resting calmly on it, posture patient with the passage of time.
CAT11-020.sceneLine: A soft red accent line runs beside the figure, brighter where it begins and softening to pale red further along, suggesting how a stress response may ease or persist as time unfolds.

CAT11-021.headline: Fight Flight Freeze Fawn
CAT11-021.subtitle: Understanding trauma survival responses
CAT11-021.figureLine: Four clay figures stand in a quiet semicircle, each in a distinct settled posture, conveying four protective instincts shown side by side without alarm.
CAT11-021.sceneLine: Four soft light red forms of different shapes rest before the figures, all glowing in the same calm pale red, suggesting four survival responses that are each understandable and wise.

CAT11-023.headline: The Freeze Response
CAT11-023.subtitle: How trauma triggers nervous system shutdown
CAT11-023.figureLine: A single clay figure stands very still with arms held close, posture paused and held, just beginning to soften at the knees.
CAT11-023.sceneLine: A held, quiet soft red accent stillness surrounds the figure and a single pale red thread begins to flow again at its feet, suggesting a protective pause that can gently start to thaw.

CAT11-024.headline: Tonic Immobility
CAT11-024.subtitle: Why trauma survivors freeze
CAT11-024.figureLine: A single reclining clay figure rests motionless and low to the ground, one hand only now beginning to lift, posture held still by instinct and slowly easing.
CAT11-024.sceneLine: A still soft light red glow blankets the resting figure and a faint pale red warmth returns first to its lifting hand, suggesting an involuntary stillness gently giving way to movement.

CAT11-025.headline: Trauma Triggers
CAT11-025.subtitle: What they are and how to manage them
CAT11-025.figureLine: A single clay figure stands calmly beside a small spark-like form, one open hand held steadily toward it without flinching, posture grounded and prepared.
CAT11-025.sceneLine: A small bright soft red accent point flares near the figure and softens to manageable pale red within its steady open hand, suggesting a trigger met with awareness and calm.

CAT11-026.headline: Trauma Avoidance
CAT11-026.subtitle: How avoidance keeps PTSD alive
CAT11-026.figureLine: A single clay figure stands half-turned from a covered form yet glancing back toward it, posture caught between turning away and beginning to face it.
CAT11-026.sceneLine: A soft red accent shape sits partly veiled beside the figure with one pale red edge gently uncovered, suggesting what is avoided slowly becoming safe to turn toward.

CAT11-027.headline: Trauma Bonding And Abuse
CAT11-027.subtitle: Why leaving abuse feels impossible
CAT11-027.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with one foot already stepping outward while a tether at its waist begins to loosen, posture leaning toward freedom.
CAT11-027.sceneLine: A soft red accent cord around the figure's waist slackens and frays into pale red wisps as it steps away across the dark red ground, suggesting a powerful pull that can finally give way.

CAT11-028.headline: Why Survivors Blame Themselves
CAT11-028.subtitle: Self-blame after trauma
CAT11-028.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with a heavy rounded form pressed against its own chest, one hand just beginning to lift it away, posture releasing an undeserved weight.
CAT11-028.sceneLine: A dense soft red accent form rests over the figure's chest and lightens to pale red as its hand starts to draw it off, suggesting misplaced blame gently set down where it was never owed.

CAT11-029.headline: Anger After Trauma
CAT11-029.subtitle: The emotion survivors are told not to feel
CAT11-029.figureLine: A single clay figure stands tall with one steady hand pressed firmly to the earth, posture firm and grounded, conveying a strong feeling held with dignity.
CAT11-029.sceneLine: A warm soft red accent glow rises through the figure and settles into a steady pale red around its grounded hand, suggesting anger acknowledged as valid and safely held.

CAT11-030.headline: Hyperarousal
CAT11-030.subtitle: Living with a nervous system stuck on alert
CAT11-030.figureLine: A single clay figure stands tensed and upright with shoulders raised, beginning to exhale and lower them, posture wound tight and slowly releasing.
CAT11-030.sceneLine: Tight soft red accent lines vibrate close around the figure then slacken into looser pale red curves as it exhales, suggesting a system stuck on high alert finally easing down.

CAT11-031.headline: Somatic Experiencing
CAT11-031.subtitle: A body-based approach to healing trauma
CAT11-031.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with both hands resting gently along its own sides, weight rocking subtly into balance, posture attentive and at home in its body.
CAT11-031.sceneLine: A soft red accent wave moves slowly up through the figure and discharges into a calm pale red ripple at its feet, suggesting stored activation gently completing and releasing through the body.

CAT11-032.headline: EMDR Explained
CAT11-032.subtitle: How eye movements process traumatic memories
CAT11-032.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests calmly with its head turning slowly and evenly from side to side, posture composed and quietly processing.
CAT11-032.sceneLine: A soft light red point glides gently back and forth in a smooth arc before the figure, leaving a settling pale red trail, suggesting difficult memories being gradually processed and filed away.

CAT11-033.headline: Trauma-Sensitive Yoga
CAT11-033.subtitle: Reconnecting with the body after trauma
CAT11-033.figureLine: A single clay figure rests in a gentle grounded pose with both hands open and weight settled low, posture safe, voluntary, and reconnecting.
CAT11-033.sceneLine: A soft red accent breath of warmth moves slowly through the figure and pools into reassuring pale red beneath it, suggesting a careful, chosen return to feeling at home in the body.

CAT11-034.headline: The Vagus Nerve
CAT11-034.subtitle: Activating the calm system in trauma recovery
CAT11-034.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with one hand resting softly at its throat and chest, breathing slow and even, posture deliberately calming itself.
CAT11-034.sceneLine: A soft red accent line winds gently from the figure's throat down through its core and eases into a calm pale red glow, suggesting the body's own calming pathway being gently switched on.

CAT11-035.headline: Breathwork For Trauma
CAT11-035.subtitle: Regulating a dysregulated nervous system
CAT11-035.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests with chest gently rising, hands open on its knees, posture centered in slow, deliberate breathing.
CAT11-035.sceneLine: A soft light red glow expands and contracts in a slow even rhythm around the figure's chest and settles into steady pale red, suggesting breath bringing a dysregulated system back into balance.

CAT11-036.headline: Neurofeedback For PTSD
CAT11-036.subtitle: Training the brain after trauma
CAT11-036.figureLine: A single seated clay figure sits attentively still, hands resting calmly, posture focused and patiently learning a new inner rhythm.
CAT11-036.sceneLine: An uneven soft red accent waveform near the figure's head gradually smooths into a calm, even pale red line, suggesting the brain gently being trained back toward steadiness.

CAT11-037.headline: Sensorimotor Psychotherapy
CAT11-037.subtitle: Healing trauma through the body
CAT11-037.figureLine: A single clay figure stands mid-gesture completing a slow, intentional movement with one arm, posture finishing what the body once held back.
CAT11-037.sceneLine: A soft red accent arc traces the figure's slow movement and resolves into a complete, settled pale red curve, suggesting an unfinished protective impulse gently allowed to complete.

CAT11-038.headline: Nature And Ecotherapy
CAT11-038.subtitle: How nature supports trauma recovery
CAT11-038.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with bare feet planted on open ground and arms loosely open, posture grounded and quietly restored by open space.
CAT11-038.sceneLine: Soft light red organic forms like simple leaves and grasses rise gently around the figure and lighten to pale red overhead, suggesting the steadying, restorative presence of the natural world.

CAT11-039.headline: Animal-Assisted Therapy
CAT11-039.subtitle: Trauma recovery with dogs and horses
CAT11-039.figureLine: A single clay figure crouches gently with one open hand extended toward a small calm rounded companion form beside it, posture trusting and at ease.
CAT11-039.sceneLine: A warm soft red accent glow rests between the figure's open hand and the small companion form and settles into gentle pale red, suggesting safe, wordless connection supporting recovery.

CAT11-040.headline: When Talk Isn't Enough
CAT11-040.subtitle: Why traditional talk therapy often falls short for trauma
CAT11-040.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with one hand near its mouth and the other resting on its own body, posture discovering that healing reaches beyond words.
CAT11-040.sceneLine: A faint soft red accent wisp drifts up near the figure's head while a fuller pale red glow gathers through its whole body, suggesting that words alone reach less than the body itself.

CAT11-041.headline: Trauma-Informed Care
CAT11-041.subtitle: Principles that transform service delivery
CAT11-041.figureLine: A single clay figure stands sheltered within a calm protective curve while another figure rests a steadying hand nearby, posture safe and respectfully supported.
CAT11-041.sceneLine: A gentle soft red accent arc curves protectively around both figures and softens to reassuring pale red, suggesting care designed first and foremost to feel safe.

CAT11-043.headline: Trauma And Trust
CAT11-043.subtitle: How trauma disrupts attachment and intimacy
CAT11-043.figureLine: Two clay figures stand a careful distance apart, each beginning to reach a single hand toward the other, posture cautious and tenderly rebuilding trust.
CAT11-043.sceneLine: A frayed soft red accent thread between the two figures slowly mends into a continuous pale red strand as their hands draw near, suggesting connection that can be carefully rebuilt.

CAT11-045.headline: Supporting A Partner With PTSD
CAT11-045.subtitle: What helps and what backfires
CAT11-045.figureLine: Two clay figures stand close, one resting a calm steadying hand on the other's back while leaving them room to breathe, posture patient and attuned.
CAT11-045.sceneLine: A soft red accent warmth passes gently from the supporting figure and settles into a calm pale red glow around the other, suggesting steady, unhurried support that genuinely helps.

CAT11-047.headline: Workplace Trauma
CAT11-047.subtitle: Harassment, accidents, and hidden psychological injury
CAT11-047.figureLine: A single clay figure stands having set down a heavy rounded form, one hand resting on its own chest, posture acknowledging an injury that was long unseen.
CAT11-047.sceneLine: A hidden soft red accent form beside the figure becomes gently visible in clear pale red light, suggesting an unseen psychological injury finally recognized and tended.

CAT11-048.headline: Trauma-Informed Healthcare
CAT11-048.subtitle: Why ACEs shape medical experiences
CAT11-048.figureLine: A single clay figure rests calmly within a sheltering space while another figure stands attentively nearby, posture safe, respected, and at ease.
CAT11-048.sceneLine: A soft red accent arc forms a gentle protective canopy over the resting figure and warms to pale red, suggesting care shaped to feel safe rather than frightening.
